title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: rstatic provides composable primitives build serialize static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) documents. static catalog tree plain JSON files linked one another, following STAC specification version 1.0.0: vignette walks building tree scratch. output written temporary directory vignette fully self-contained.
code: stac/   catalog.json   collections/     {collection}/       collection.json       items/         {item}/           item.json root <- tempfile("stac-")

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: stac_init() creates (updates) root catalog.json. preserves existing child links, safe call repeatedly.
code: catalog <- stac_init(   id = "restore-plus",   title = "Restore+ Catalog",   description = "An example static STAC catalog built with rstatic",   root_dir = root ) #> Catalog restore-plus initialized/updated at /tmp/RtmpKNxIUB/stac-1e7a787f00a6/stac/catalog.json catalog #> <STAC Catalog> #>   id:    restore-plus #>   title: Restore+ Catalog #>   links: 2

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: Collections group related items. new_collection() builds document memory stac_add_collection() persists links catalog. extra named arguments (e.g. citation, doi) stored additional collection fields.
code: collection <- stac_add_collection(   catalog,   collection = new_collection(     id = "land-cover",     title = "Example Land Cover",     description = "Annual land cover maps (example data)",     license = "CC-BY-4.0"   ),   root_dir = root ) #> Collection land-cover added to Catalog. collection #> <STAC Collection> #>   id:    land-cover #>   title: Example Land Cover #>   links: 3

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: terra installed, extract_bbox() reads raster returns bounding box WGS84. small Sentinel-2 subset ships package demonstration. terra available, can pass bounding box directly. as_geometry() helper turns bounding box GeoJSON polygon external dependencies:
code: tif <- system.file("extdata/example.tif", package = "rstatic") bbox <- extract_bbox(tif) bbox #>       xmin       ymin       xmax       ymax  #> -63.636579  -8.630220 -63.450282  -8.491029 bbox <- c(-50, -10, -49, -9) geometry <- as_geometry(bbox) str(geometry, max.level = 2) #> List of 2 #>  $ type       : chr "Polygon" #>  $ coordinates:List of 1 #>   ..$ :List of 5

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: Items GeoJSON Features. Build properties new_properties() assets new_asset(); asset media type deduced file extension.
code: item <- new_item(   id = "land-cover-2020",   bbox = bbox,   geometry = geometry,   properties = new_properties(     datetime = "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z",     description = "Example land cover map for 2020"   ),   assets = list(     data = new_asset("land-cover-2020.tif", title = "Land Cover 2020")   ) ) item #> <STAC Item> #>   id:     land-cover-2020 #>   assets: 1 #>   links:  4

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: stac_add_items() writes item, links collection, updates collection’s spatial temporal extent automatically.
code: collection <- stac_add_items(collection, item, root_dir = root) #> Added 1 item(s) to collection land-cover. collection$extent$spatial$bbox[[1]] #> [1] -50 -10 -49  -9 collection$extent$temporal$interval[[1]] #> [[1]] #> [1] "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z" #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z"

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: terra, new_thumbnail() renders PNG preview. Styles can built explicitly stac_style() parsed QGIS .qml file qml_to_style() (requires xml2).
code: style <- stac_style(min = 0, max = 255, palette = c("black", "white")) style$type #> [1] "simple" thumb <- new_thumbnail(   collection_id = "land-cover",   item_id = "land-cover-2020",   asset_href = system.file("extdata/example.tif", package = "rstatic"),   width = 200,   root_dir = root ) thumb$roles #> [[1]] #> [1] "thumbnail"

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: final directory tree contains linked JSON documents:
code: list.files(file.path(root, "stac"), recursive = TRUE) #> [1] "catalog.json"                                               #> [2] "collections/land-cover/collection.json"                     #> [3] "collections/land-cover/items/land-cover-2020/item.json"     #> [4] "collections/land-cover/items/land-cover-2020/thumbnail.png"

title: Authors and Citation
text: Rolf Simoes. Author, maintainer. Felipe Carlos. Author. Felipe Souza. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Simoes R, Carlos F, Souza F (2026). rstatic: Primitives Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/rolfsimoes/rstatic.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rstatic: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs},   author = {Rolf Simoes and Felipe Carlos and Felipe Souza},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/rolfsimoes/rstatic}, }

what: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs
title: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs
text: Primitives generate static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs (STAC). STAC specification files web services used describe geospatial information assets. specification can consulted https://stacspec.org/. rstatic provides small, composable primitives build serialize static STAC documents (catalogs, collections, items, assets, links). designed reusable foundation packages (e.g. STAC generators, experiment catalog builders) can rely . implements STAC specification version 1.0.0. Optional features rely suggested packages degrade gracefully installed: terra — bounding-box extraction (extract_bbox()) thumbnail rendering (new_thumbnail()). xml2 — QGIS style parsing (qml_to_style()). packages unavailable, can still build documents supplying bounding boxes, geometries, styles manually.

title: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs
text: can install development version rstatic GitHub :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("rolfsimoes/rstatic")

title: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs
text: example builds minimal static catalog one collection one item, writing JSON tree temporary directory. resulting directory follows canonical static catalog layout:
code: library(rstatic)  root <- tempfile("stac-")  # 1. Initialize the root catalog catalog <- stac_init(   id = "example",   title = "Example Catalog",   description = "A minimal static STAC catalog",   root_dir = root ) #> Catalog example initialized/updated at /tmp/RtmpMajh8n/stac-2dcc367caca80/stac/catalog.json  # 2. Add a collection collection <- stac_add_collection(   catalog,   collection = new_collection(     id = "land-cover",     title = "Land Cover",     description = "Example land cover collection"   ),   root_dir = root ) #> Collection land-cover added to Catalog.  # 3. Build and add an item item <- new_item(   id = "land-cover-2020",   bbox = c(-50, -10, -49, -9),   properties = new_properties(datetime = "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z"),   assets = list(     data = new_asset("land-cover-2020.tif", title = "Land Cover 2020")   ) )  collection <- stac_add_items(collection, item, root_dir = root) #> Added 1 item(s) to collection land-cover. list.files(file.path(root, "stac"), recursive = TRUE) #> [1] "catalog.json"                                           #> [2] "collections/land-cover/collection.json"                 #> [3] "collections/land-cover/items/land-cover-2020/item.json"

title: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs

what: Create and initialize STAC catalogs — catalog_functions
title: Create and initialize STAC catalogs — catalog_functions
text: Functions build STAC Catalog document. new_catalog(): creates -memory Catalog object. stac_init(): creates (updates) root catalog disk stac/ directory, preserving existing child links.

title: Create and initialize STAC catalogs — catalog_functions
code: new_catalog(id, title, description, stac_version = "1.0.0", ...)  stac_init(id, title, description, root_dir = ".")

title: Create and initialize STAC catalogs — catalog_functions
text: id character identifier catalog. title character human-readable title. description character description catalog. stac_version character STAC specification version. Defaults "1.0.0". ... Additional named fields add catalog document. root_dir character directory catalog written. Defaults current working directory.

title: Create and initialize STAC catalogs — catalog_functions
text: new_catalog(): doc_catalog object. stac_init(): invisibly, saved doc_catalog object.

title: Create and initialize STAC catalogs — catalog_functions
code: cat <- new_catalog(   id = "my-catalog",   title = "My Catalog",   description = "An example STAC catalog" ) cat$type #> [1] "Catalog"  # Write a root catalog to a temporary directory dir <- tempfile("stac-") stac_init(   id = "my-catalog",   title = "My Catalog",   description = "An example STAC catalog",   root_dir = dir ) #> Catalog my-catalog initialized/updated at /tmp/Rtmp5LCnvu/stac-1d272db2788/stac/catalog.json

what: Create and register STAC collections — collection_functions
title: Create and register STAC collections — collection_functions
text: Functions build attach STAC Collection document. new_collection(): creates -memory Collection object. stac_add_collection(): persists collection registers child catalog, saves updated catalog.

title: Create and register STAC collections — collection_functions
code: new_collection(   id,   title,   description,   license = "proprietary",   extent = NULL,   stac_version = "1.0.0",   ... )  stac_add_collection(catalog, collection = NULL, ..., root_dir = ".")

title: Create and register STAC collections — collection_functions
text: id character identifier collection. title character human-readable title. description character description collection. license character license identifier URL. Defaults "proprietary". extent list collection spatial temporal extent. NULL, empty extent created later updated stac_add_items(). stac_version character STAC specification version. Defaults "1.0.0". ... Additional named fields. new_collection(), added collection document. stac_add_collection(), passed new_collection() collection NULL. catalog doc_catalog object collection attached . collection doc_collection object register. NULL, one created .... root_dir character directory documents written. Defaults current working directory.

title: Create and register STAC collections — collection_functions
text: new_collection(): doc_collection object. stac_add_collection(): invisibly, saved doc_collection.

title: Create and register STAC collections — collection_functions
code: col <- new_collection(   id = "my-collection",   title = "My Collection",   description = "An example collection" ) col$type #> [1] "Collection"  dir <- tempfile("stac-") cat <- stac_init("cat", "Catalog", "Example", root_dir = dir) #> Catalog cat initialized/updated at /tmp/Rtmp5LCnvu/stac-1d27561f91a5/stac/catalog.json stac_add_collection(cat, collection = col, root_dir = dir) #> Collection my-collection added to Catalog.

what: Spatial helpers for STAC documents — geom_functions
title: Spatial helpers for STAC documents — geom_functions
text: Helper functions derive spatial metadata required STAC items. extract_bbox(): reads raster (local remote) returns bounding box WGS84 (EPSG:4326). function requires optional terra package. as_geometry(): converts bounding box GeoJSON Polygon geometry. function external dependencies. terra installed, can still build items passing bounding box (numeric vector c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)) directly new_item() , optionally, geometry created as_geometry().

title: Spatial helpers for STAC documents — geom_functions
code: extract_bbox(url)  as_geometry(bbox)

title: Spatial helpers for STAC documents — geom_functions
text: url character path URL raster file readable terra. Remote http(s) URLs accessed GDAL's /vsicurl/ driver. bbox numeric vector length 4 bounding box coordinates order c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax).

title: Spatial helpers for STAC documents — geom_functions
text: extract_bbox(): numeric vector c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax), NULL raster read. as_geometry(): list representing GeoJSON Polygon geometry, NULL bbox NULL.

title: Spatial helpers for STAC documents — geom_functions
code: # as_geometry() works without any optional dependency bbox <- c(-50, -10, -49, -9) as_geometry(bbox) #> $type #> [1] "Polygon" #>  #> $coordinates #> $coordinates[[1]] #> $coordinates[[1]][[1]] #> [1] -50 -10 #>  #> $coordinates[[1]][[2]] #> [1] -49 -10 #>  #> $coordinates[[1]][[3]] #> [1] -49  -9 #>  #> $coordinates[[1]][[4]] #> [1] -50  -9 #>  #> $coordinates[[1]][[5]] #> [1] -50 -10 #>  #>  #>   # extract_bbox() requires terra if (requireNamespace("terra", quietly = TRUE)) {   f <- system.file("extdata/example.tif", package = "rstatic")   if (nzchar(f)) {     extract_bbox(f)   } } #>       xmin       ymin       xmax       ymax  #> -63.636579  -8.630220 -63.450282  -8.491029

what: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
title: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
text: Functions build building blocks STAC Item. new_properties(): assembles item properties list. new_asset(): creates single STAC Asset. new_item(): creates -memory Item (GeoJSON Feature). stac_add_items(): persists one items collection, updating collection links spatio-temporal extent.

title: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
code: new_properties(   description = NULL,   datetime = NULL,   start_datetime = NULL,   end_datetime = NULL,   start_date = NULL,   end_date = NULL,   ... )  new_asset(href, title = NULL, roles = list("data"), ...)  new_item(   id,   bbox,   geometry = NULL,   properties = new_properties(),   assets = list(),   stac_version = "1.0.0",   ... )  stac_add_items(collection, ..., root_dir = ".")

title: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
text: description character description. datetime character RFC 3339 datetime, NULL. start_datetime character RFC 3339 start datetime, NULL. end_datetime character RFC 3339 end datetime, NULL. start_date character start date, NULL. end_date character end date, NULL. ... Additional named fields. See details function. href character asset target (path URL). title character title. roles list asset roles. Defaults list("data"). id character identifier item. bbox numeric vector c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). geometry GeoJSON geometry list. NULL, derived bbox via as_geometry(). properties list item properties, e.g. new_properties(). assets named list assets, e.g. new_asset(). stac_version character STAC version. Defaults "1.0.0". collection doc_collection object add items . root_dir character directory documents written. Defaults current working directory.

title: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
text: new_properties(): list properties. new_asset(): list describing asset. new_item(): doc_item object. stac_add_items(): invisibly, updated doc_collection.

title: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
code: props <- new_properties(datetime = "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z") asset <- new_asset("data.tif", title = "Data") item <- new_item(   id = "item-1",   bbox = c(-50, -10, -49, -9),   properties = props,   assets = list(data = asset) ) item$type #> [1] "Feature"  dir <- tempfile("stac-") cat <- stac_init("cat", "Catalog", "Example", root_dir = dir) #> Catalog cat initialized/updated at /tmp/Rtmp5LCnvu/stac-1d271bf62351/stac/catalog.json col <- stac_add_collection(   cat,   collection = new_collection("col", "Collection", "Example"),   root_dir = dir ) #> Collection col added to Catalog. stac_add_items(col, item, root_dir = dir) #> Added 1 item(s) to collection col.

title: Generate a thumbnail asset — new_thumbnail
text: Renders PNG thumbnail raster returns STAC Asset pointing . thumbnail written canonical item directory stac/collections/{collection_id}/items/{item_id}/thumbnail.png. function requires optional terra package. terra installed, build thumbnail asset manually new_asset().

title: Generate a thumbnail asset — new_thumbnail
code: new_thumbnail(   collection_id,   item_id,   asset_href,   width = 800,   title = "Thumbnail",   style = NULL,   root_dir = ".",   ... )

title: Generate a thumbnail asset — new_thumbnail
text: collection_id character collection identifier. item_id character item identifier. asset_href character path URL source raster. width integer thumbnail width pixels. Defaults 800. title character asset title. Defaults "Thumbnail". style optional style list stac_style() qml_to_style(). root_dir character directory thumbnail written. Defaults current working directory. ... Additional arguments passed terra::plot().

title: Generate a thumbnail asset — new_thumbnail
text: list describing thumbnail asset (new_asset()).

title: Generate a thumbnail asset — new_thumbnail
code: if (requireNamespace("terra", quietly = TRUE)) {   f <- system.file("extdata/example.tif", package = "rstatic")   if (nzchar(f)) {     dir <- tempfile("stac-")     new_thumbnail(       collection_id = "col",       item_id = "item-1",       asset_href = f,       root_dir = dir     )   } } #> $href #> [1] "thumbnail.png" #>  #> $type #> [1] "image/png" #>  #> $roles #> $roles[[1]] #> [1] "thumbnail" #>  #>  #> $title #> [1] "Thumbnail" #>

title: Print STAC documents — print_rstatic
text: Compact S3 print methods rstatic STAC documents.

title: Print STAC documents — print_rstatic
code: # S3 method for class 'doc_catalog' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'doc_collection' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'doc_item' print(x, ...)

title: Print STAC documents — print_rstatic
text: x doc_catalog, doc_collection, doc_item object. ... Additional arguments (currently ignored).

title: Print STAC documents — print_rstatic
text: Invisibly, x.

title: Print STAC documents — print_rstatic
code: print(new_catalog("c", "Catalog", "An example catalog")) #> <STAC Catalog> #>   id:    c #>   title: Catalog #>   links: 2 print(new_collection("col", "Collection", "An example collection")) #> <STAC Collection> #>   id:    col #>   title: Collection #>   links: 3

what: rstatic: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs — rstatic-package
title: rstatic: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs — rstatic-package
text: Provides reusable primitives build serialize static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) documents, including catalogs, collections, items, assets, links. package implements STAC specification version 1.0.0 (https://github.com/radiantearth/stac-spec) intended foundation tools, STAC generators experiment catalog builders. Optional spatial metadata extraction thumbnail rendering supported 'terra', 'QGIS' style parsing 'xml2', packages available.

title: rstatic: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs — rstatic-package
text: Maintainer: Rolf Simoes rolfsimoes@gmail.com Authors: Rolf Simoes rolfsimoes@gmail.com Felipe Carlos efelipecarlos@gmail.com Felipe Souza lipecaso@gmail.com

what: Save a STAC document to its canonical path — stac_save
title: Save a STAC document to its canonical path — stac_save
text: Writes STAC Catalog, Collection, Item disk following canonical static catalog layout: Catalog    -> stac/catalog.json Collection -> stac/collections/{id}/collection.json Item       -> stac/collections/{collection}/items/{id}/item.json Items must carry collection field path can resolved.

title: Save a STAC document to its canonical path — stac_save
code: stac_save(obj, root_dir = ".")

title: Save a STAC document to its canonical path — stac_save
text: obj doc_catalog, doc_collection, doc_item object. root_dir character directory stac/ tree written. Defaults current working directory.

title: Save a STAC document to its canonical path — stac_save
text: Invisibly, saved object (re-classed rstatic document).

title: Save a STAC document to its canonical path — stac_save
code: dir <- tempfile("stac-") cat <- new_catalog("c", "Catalog", "An example catalog") stac_save(cat, root_dir = dir)

what: STAC style objects for thumbnails — style_functions
title: STAC style objects for thumbnails — style_functions
text: Helpers describe raster rendered thumbnail. stac_style(): builds style object explicit parameters. function external dependencies. qml_to_style(): parses QGIS Layer Style file (.qml) style object. function requires optional xml2 package. xml2 installed, build style manually stac_style() instead reading .qml file.

title: STAC style objects for thumbnails — style_functions
code: stac_style(   min = NULL,   max = NULL,   pmin = NULL,   pmax = NULL,   palette = NULL,   legend = NULL )  qml_to_style(qml_path)

title: STAC style objects for thumbnails — style_functions
text: min Minimum value stretching color mapping. max Maximum value stretching color mapping. pmin Percentile minimum stretching (e.g. 0.02). pmax Percentile maximum stretching (e.g. 0.98). palette Color palette name vector colors. legend data.frame value color columns categorical data. qml_path character path URL QGIS .qml style file.

title: STAC style objects for thumbnails — style_functions
text: list style parameters: categorical, continuous, simple style depending inputs.

title: STAC style objects for thumbnails — style_functions
code: # Build a style directly (no optional dependency required) stac_style(min = 0, max = 255, palette = c("black", "white")) #> $type #> [1] "simple" #>  #> $min #> [1] 0 #>  #> $max #> [1] 255 #>  #> $palette #> [1] "black" "white" #>   # Parse a QML file (requires xml2) if (requireNamespace("xml2", quietly = TRUE)) {   qml <- system.file("extdata/example.qml", package = "rstatic")   if (nzchar(qml)) {     qml_to_style(qml)   } } #> $type #> [1] "categorical" #>  #> $legend #>   value     color #> 1     1 #1f78b4FF #> 2     2 #33a02cFF #> 3     3 #e31a1cFF #>
